column2: # Use a colorblind-friendly color palette (from RColorBrewer or viridis)
# You can use a friendly palette like this one:
colors <- c("conformed to\nconfederate" = "#56B4E9",    # Sky Blue
"did not conform\nto confederate" = "#E69F00", # Amber
"changed opinion,\nconfederate neutral" = "#009E73",  # Soft Green
"no change in\nopinion" = "#999999")
agreement_dist$agreement <- factor(agreement_dist$agreement,

levels = c("conformed to
confederate", "did not conform
to confederate", "changed opinion,
confederate neutral", "no change in
opinion"))
# Plot the stacked bar graph for agreement, disagreement, and neutral
ggplot(agreement_dist, aes(x = cond_eng, y = percentage, fill = agreement)) +
geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
labs(
y = "Percentage (%)",
fill = "Opinion Change") +
theme_minimal() +
scale_fill_manual(values = colors) +  # Apply the custom color palette
theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1),
legend.position = "right",            # Position legend on the right side
legend.box = "vertical",               # Legend items in a vertical box
legend.text = element_text(size = 12), # Decrease legend text size
legend.title = element_text(size = 12), # Increase legend title size
axis.title = element_text(size = 14),  # Increase axis title size
axis.text = element_text(size = 12),    # Increase axis text size
plot.margin = margin(10, 10, 10, 10),
legend.key.height = unit(2, "lines"),         # Increase height of legend keys
legend.spacing.y = unit(0.2, "lines")# Adjust margins to give more space on the right
guides(fill = guide_legend(nrow = 4))
